ai_strategy.summary: University of Idaho has public AI evidence across 6 trend categories, including governance, teaching, research, tools, or workforce activity.

key_provisions: Use reviewed tools for sensitive data.
Follow instructor or unit AI expectations.
Keep human accountability for consequential decisions.

initiatives.title: AI @ UI campus portal
initiatives.description: The AI @ UI portal organizes AI events, teaching, administration, literacy, ethics, research opportunities, and news.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.title: AI for Research Administration
initiatives.description: UI's AI4RA project aims to democratize access to AI-enabled tools and data resources for research administration and technology transfer.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

initiatives.title: Graduate AI degree program
initiatives.description: University of Idaho catalog materials describe AI M.S. and M.Engr. programs that train students to assess and integrate emerging AI research and tools.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.title: AI professional learning series
initiatives.description: UI's Center for Excellence in Teaching and Learning offers an AI professional learning series on generative AI in teaching and learning.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

initiatives.title: AI courses and certificate
initiatives.description: UI public materials list AI courses and an AI/ML graduate academic certificate, supporting workforce-ready AI skills.

initiatives.title: Kestrel 24-GPU HPC cluster
initiatives.description: University of Idaho Research Computing publishes Kestrel cluster details showing 24 AI-capable GPUs: 8 NVIDIA A100 and 16 NVIDIA L40S accelerators.
initiatives.significance: The exact GPU count closes a compute-capacity gap for Idaho.

investment.description: Research Computing publishes GPU inventory but not capital cost.

research_centers.name: AI research or coordination activity
research_centers.focus: Public AI research, teaching, or tool coordination evidenced in cited sources.

computing_infrastructure.summary: University of Idaho Research Computing lists Kestrel as a shared HPC cluster with 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 16 NVIDIA L40S GPUs, plus CPU and storage resources for AI-capable research workloads.

narrative_summary: University of Idaho shows an AI posture with five documented initiatives across 6 categories. The evidence emphasizes AI @ UI campus portal, AI for Research Administration, and Graduate AI degree program. Policy maturity is coded as permissive based on public guidance, privacy language, governance, or tool controls. The profile also records teaching, research, workforce, equity, or enterprise-tool activity where sources support it. Leadership names, enrollment, GPU counts, and contract values are left null when not found in the cited public sources.

gaps_acknowledged: Leadership names and enrollment were not populated in this missing-only sweep.
Detailed AI budgets, user counts, and GPU counts were not consistently public.
No controversy record was added without a reliable institution-specific source.
University of Idaho publishes Kestrel GPU inventory and NSF AI funding signals, but no campus-wide enterprise AI platform user counts or contract values were found.
No public student-subgroup outcome metrics tied to Idaho AI deployments surfaced in this sweep.
